title: Fix JENKINS-35272: Prevent afterDisconnect() from being called twice
issue_text:
Added AtomicBoolean flag to ensure afterDisconnect() is only called once per disconnect cycle. The flag is reset when a new connection is established via setChannel(). This prevents duplicate executions when an agent disconnects, which was causing issues like VMs shutting down twice.

Fixes #17204

## Root Cause
The `afterDisconnect()` method was being called in two places:
1. In the channel listener's `onClosed()` callback (line ~654) - triggered automatically when channel closes
2. In the explicit `disconnect()` method (line ~826) - called during manual disconnects

When a manual disconnect occurred, `closeChannel()` would trigger the listener's `onClosed()` callback, causing both code paths to execute and `afterDisconnect()` to run twice.

## Solution
Added a thread-safe `AtomicBoolean` flag (`afterDisconnectCalled`) that:
- Uses `compareAndSet(false, true)` to ensure only the first caller succeeds
- Is reset to `false` when a new connection is established in `setChannel()`
- Prevents duplicate execution while maintaining thread safety for concurrent disconnect scenarios

### Testing done

- Built successfully with `mvn -am -pl war,bom -Pquick-build clean install`
- All unit tests pass with `mvn test -Plight-test`
- Code formatting verified with `mvn spotless:check`
- Manually verified the flag logic:
  - Flag is set to false on new connection
  - First `afterDisconnect()` call succeeds (flag changes from false to true)
  - Second `afterDisconnect()` call is blocked (flag is already true)

### Screenshots (UI changes only)

N/A - This is a backend logic fix with no UI changes

### Proposed changelog entries

- Prevent `afterDisconnect()` from being called twice when an agent disconnects

### Proposed changelog category

/label bug
